message:
feat(packs): support \${repo_root} substitution in custom_paths

Lets dcg policy packs that ship via global config (e.g. MDM-deployed
~/.config/dcg/config.toml) auto-discover per-repo policy fragments
without forcing every checkout to symlink them into \$HOME.

- New \`PacksConfig::expand_custom_paths_from(cwd)\` is the testable form.
  The existing \`expand_custom_paths\` keeps its zero-arg signature and
  delegates to the new function with \`std::env::current_dir().ok()\`.
- Pattern handling now does \${repo_root} expansion *first*, then tilde,
  then glob — so users can write entries like
  \`\${repo_root}/.dcg/packs/**/*.toml\` or \`\${repo_root}/policy/local.toml\`.
- \${repo_root} is resolved by walking up from the cwd (capped at
  \`REPO_ROOT_SEARCH_MAX_HOPS\` = 50) looking for the nearest ancestor
  that contains a \`.git\` directory. The result is memoized inside the
  function so multiple repo-rooted entries don't re-traverse.
- When no repo root is found (running outside any git checkout), entries
  containing \${repo_root} are *silently skipped* rather than errored.
  This is the right behavior for fail-open packs deployed globally:
  the config is harmless on a CI box / on a machine where the user is
  cd'd into \$HOME, and the existing tilde/glob branches keep working.
- Doc comment is updated to call out the new token, the \`.git\`-discovery
  semantics, and the silent-skip-on-no-repo behavior so operators can
  reason about deployment.

No behavior change for configs that don't use \${repo_root}.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
